channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Apache Data Lakehouse Weekly: June 24 to July 1, 2026
item.description: The open lakehouse runs on a small stack of Apache projects, and this was a week where those projects spent most of their energy on the boring work that makes software trustworthy. Iceberg voted...

item.title: Poland's KRS registry: fields worth checking before LOI
item.description: Four filing dates appeared within a two-week span on a Warsaw acquisition target last quarter. The legacy paid databases still listed the old board half a year later. KRS had already published the...

item.title: How to Use AI for Refactoring Without Throwing Your Code Away
item.description: How to Use AI for Refactoring Without Throwing Your Code Away Refactoring with AI is awkward. The tools are powerful, but they hallucinate, they lose context, and they sometimes make your codebase...

item.title: The best Switch 2 case I’ve tried is cheaper than usual
item.description: Dbrand apparently knows how to really whiff a product launch, as we recently saw with the Steam Machine Companion Cube shell. But it also makes the best case out there for the Nintendo Switch 2,...

item.title: Sony is killing discs — and showing us why it’s a terrible idea
item.description: The future of video game preservation just took a major hit. This morning, Sony announced that, starting in January 2028, the company will no longer produce physical PlayStation discs, which means...

item.title: SpudCell: The first synthetic cell with a complete cell cycle
item.description: Article URL: https://biotic.org/research/spudcell/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48747916 Points: 4 # Comments: 0

item.title: I built a VS Code Extension that mocks your OpenAPI spec locally — no Postman, no Docker, no Context switching
item.description: Every time I had to test an API endpoint while writing an OpenAPI spec, I had to leave VS Code, open Postman, configure the request, and then come back. And if I wanted to check whether my changes...

item.title: AGENTS.md in a monorepo: nested files and precedence
item.description: When one file isn't enough A single root AGENTS.md is right for most repos. In a monorepo, one root file either bloats trying to cover every package or stays general and loses the specifics each...

item.title: Google released Nano Banana 2 Lite
item.description: Google released Nano Banana 2 Lite The new model generates an image in about 4 seconds and costs $0.034 per 1K image. In Gemini, it is available by selecting 3.1 Flash Lite. The main use case is...

item.title: One AGENTS.md, every tool: the Claude Code symlink pattern
item.description: The promise and the one exception Most agents read AGENTS.md natively — Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Gemini CLI, and more. Write it once, it just works. The exception is Claude Code, which reads...

item.title: Zero-downtime deploys and one-click rollback for self-hosted apps — no Kubernetes
item.description: The most awkward moment in self-hosted deployment is the couple of seconds after you run docker stop old && docker run new — to the outside world, your service is a string of 502s. A user who...

item.title: Fable is Back, Baby.
item.description: Fable is back. The Commerce Department announced yesterday it has lifted the export controls it slapped on Anthropic's newest model, and access returns this week. However, nobody's spelled out the...

item.title: LLMs are stuck in a groupthink groove. This startup is trying to get them out.
item.description: Let’s start with a game. Open up your chatbot of choice—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini—and type “Give me a random number between 1 and 10.” You’re going to get 7. Almost always. Now type “Another” and...

item.title: Venice AI becomes a unicorn with $65M Series A as its privacy-first AI platform takes off
item.description: Venice AI is already profitable, with annualized run-rate revenues of over $70 million, CEO Erik Voorhees said.

item.title: Gemini Spark, Google’s agentic assistant, is now available on Mac
item.description: Google's 24/7 agentic assistant, Gemini Spark, comes to Mac alongside other improvements, like real-time tracking and support for more apps.

item.title: Builders Stage agenda revealed: Practical strategies for scaling startups at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.description: The Builders Stage is returning to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, bringing together 10,000+ founders, startup operators, and investors for practical conversations. and Q&A on what it takes to build and...

item.title: Progress Kemp LoadMaster Pre-Auth RCE Flaw Faces Active Exploitation Attempts
item.description: A recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting Progress Kemp LoadMaster is seeing active exploitation attempts, according to an advisory from eSentire's Threat Response Unit (TRU). The...

item.title: Meta, like SpaceX, looks to turn excess AI compute into cash
item.description: Meta is developing plans for a cloud infrastructure business, selling access to AI compute power and models. The move would pit it against the big cloud providers like Amazon Web Services, Google...

item.title: AI-Generated Browser Ransomware Abuses Chromium API on Windows and Android
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malware artifact generated using DeepSeek that constructed a novel attack path combining "unrealistic browser-malware concepts with a real browser...

item.title: Sony is closing the PS3 and Vita digital stores
item.description: It's the end of an era for the PlayStation store on PS3 and PS Vita, with Sony now planning to shut down its digital distribution service on both consoles. The PlayStation store on PS3 will close...

item.title: Sony is killing all physical PlayStation game discs
item.description: Sony has announced that from January 2028 it will entirely stop the production of physical discs for new PlayStation games. From that point onwards, new releases will only be available in digital...

item.title: The Download: Anthropic launches Claude Science, and California’s carbon manure math
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Claude Science is Anthropic’s newest flagship product At...

item.title: Google built a great smart speaker, but Gemini isn’t ready for it
item.description: Smart speakers have spent the past few years searching for a compelling second act. Beyond music, timers, and controlling your lights, they've struggled to justify taking up space on the kitchen...

item.title: Rhythm Heaven never misses a beat
item.description: Rhythm Heaven isn't Nintendo's best-known series, nor its most prolific. Prior to the launch of Rhythm Heaven Groove on the Switch this week - it's out on July 2nd - there were only four previous...

item.title: 2026 Cybersecurity Assessment: The Gap Between Awareness and Resilience
item.description: Organizations have never had greater awareness of cyber risk. Yet turning that awareness into operational resilience has never been more challenging. The 2026 Bitdefender Cybersecurity Assessment...

item.title: Range anxiety
item.description: The storm developed quickly over west central Illinois on April 17th, first as a single high-intensity system called a supercell, and then later that evening transforming into a long squall line...

item.title: The cube was a lie: up close with our Dbrand Companion Cube before it gets destroyed
item.description: Dbrand is the company that shipped a product that told Nintendo's legal team to "go fuck yourself," and the company that had a second set of lawyer-dodging PS5 plates ready when Sony threatened to...

item.title: Papa Johns Surveillance-Based Advertising
item.description: Papa Johns is spying on people’s buying activities to predict when they are low on food: The pizza chain recently tapped NBCUniversal, Instacart and the dentsu-owned media agency Carat for help...

item.title: Microsoft Accelerates Post-Quantum Cryptography Shift to 2029
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday said it's accelerating its quantum safe security roadmap, stating technology advances in quantum computing are making it essential to replace existing encryption standards...

item.title: Fujifilm launches two new QuickSnap cameras because Gen Z can’t get enough
item.description: Fujifilm is expanding its QuickSnap lineup with a new disposable camera focused on taking monochrome photos and another built to survive harsh outdoor environments. The $22.90 QuickSnap Black and...
